text:
I had an unusual pet. It was a small, round owl. I named her Luna. Luna had beautiful feathers. They were a soft gray-brown. Their feathered ears pointed curiously forward. Every night, Luna would perch upon my shoulder. We would gaze into each other's eyes into darkness.

One evening I sat outside under a streetlamp. Luna nestled comfortably on my arm. I stroked her gray chest feathers lovingly. She would close her big, dark eye and snuggle closer. Just for a moment, I thought I saw something in her gaze. It was something else entirely.

It was Luna. She was looking at something small and brown hopping onto my outstretched finger. It was a rabbit. Luna had taken a genuine interest in wildlife. Occasionally she would swoop down silently, grab a mouse in one talon, and fly away. We bonded over the seasons. Every spring she became busier.

I watched as she chased after bees collecting nectar,
